scenario: Aurora Logistics scans 7200 shipments per day in Camunda 8.8. A FEEL expression must collect every reference code starting with 12 from a free-text field such as references are 1234, 1256, 1378 and return a list like [1234, 1256] for downstream routing, drawing on the documented FEEL string built-ins.
question: Which FEEL string function returns the list of all matches of a regular-expression pattern in the supplied input string?

options.text: The matches(string, pattern) function returns a boolean alongside the matching substrings, indicating whether at least one pattern hit was found
The split(string, pattern) function returns a list of substrings produced by cutting the input at every position where the pattern hits the text
The extract(string, pattern) function returns a list containing every substring of the input that matches the supplied regular expression pattern
The find(string, pattern) function returns just the first matching substring from the input and a null value when no part of the pattern hits

a.text: Incorrect. The matches function on the FEEL string built-ins page returns only a boolean that reports whether the pattern matches the input, and it does not return a list of the matching substrings drawn from the supplied text.

b.text: Incorrect. The split function cuts the input around every pattern hit and returns the surrounding pieces, which is the inverse of what extract does when it collects only the matches themselves from the supplied input.

c.text: Correct. The extract function returns a list of all substrings in the input that match the supplied regular expression pattern, and it returns an empty list when the pattern does not match anywhere in the input, matching the documented references-are-1234-1256-1378 example.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no FEEL function called find in the documented string built-ins page, and returning a single substring would not satisfy the requirement to collect every reference that starts with 12 from the shipment field.

explanation: The FEEL extract function takes an input string and a regular expression pattern, returns a list containing every substring of the input that matches the pattern, and returns an empty list when nothing matches, exactly the behavior described on the FEEL string built-ins page.
